prompt: Should we use event sourcing or CRUD for our order management system? We expect ~1000 orders/day, need audit trails, and may add a recommendation engine later.
consensus_recommendation: Use CRUD (not event sourcing) as the foundation. Both providers agree event sourcing is over-engineered for the current scale and team experience, though they differ on the specific CRUD variant.

approaches.name: CRUD with audit log
pros: Familiar to the team — Django ORM is purpose-built for CRUD
PostgreSQL triggers or Django signals can capture changes for audit
Low learning curve, fast time to market
cons: Audit log is a bolt-on, not native to the data model
Harder to replay history or reconstruct past states
May need refactoring if event-driven features grow significantly

approaches.name: CRUD with domain events
pros: Keeps the familiar Django CRUD model the team knows
Domain events provide an event stream for the recommendation engine
Audit trail via a changes table with before/after snapshots
Incremental path toward event sourcing if needed
cons: Dual-write concern: must ensure both CRUD update and event publish succeed
Audit trail is still a secondary concern
Domain events need schema versioning as the system evolves

approaches.name: Plain CRUD
cons: No audit trail without additional work
No event stream for future recommendation engine

approaches.name: Event sourcing
pros: Natural fit for temporal queries and recommendation engine
cons: Steep learning curve for the team
Django and PostgreSQL are not natural ES tools
Significantly more complex than needed at 1000 orders/day
Requires new infrastructure (event store, projections, CQRS)

summary.synthesis: Both providers strongly recommend a CRUD-based approach over event sourcing, citing the team's Django expertise and the modest order volume. They differ on whether to add domain events now (Anthropic) or keep it simpler with just an audit log (OpenAI). The domain events approach offers a clearer path to the future recommendation engine but adds dual-write complexity.
